description_long: This is a classic, upright fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and a domed top, painted in a weathered red color with visible signs of rust and wear. The hydrant features two large side outlets capped with hexagonal nuts and a central front outlet, all designed for hose attachment. The top of the hydrant is crowned with a prominent operating nut, used to open or close the water valve. The base is wide and octagonal, providing stability and anchoring the hydrant to the ground. The surface is rough and matte, with patches of exposed metal and chipped paint, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant is not shown in relation to any curb or sidewalk.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with a domed top, two side outlets, and a central front outlet, showing signs of rust and chipped paint.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central outlet and both side outlets, with the operating nut clearly visible on top.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the left side outlet and the central outlet, with the domed top and base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the hydrant and both side outlets, with no central outlet visible.
description_view_3: Angled view highlighting the right side outlet and the central outlet, with the domed top and base visible.
